text:
Your working directory is a checkout of the real react-tooltip repository at the commit where the bug below reproduces. Find and fix the bug in the library source.

Bug: with the click trigger enabled (`openOnClick`), clicking an anchor element is supposed to open its tooltip. The library implements this by listening for click events at the document level. Because that delegated listener runs during the event's bubbling phase, if the anchor's own click handler calls `event.stopPropagation()`, the click never bubbles up to the document, the library's delegated listener never runs, and the tooltip fails to open. The correct behavior is that clicking the anchor opens its tooltip even when the anchor stops propagation of the click event. Fix how/when the library registers its delegated click handling so that a propagation-stopping anchor still opens the tooltip. Fix the library source (under `src/`); do not just add workarounds in test files.

Environment notes: no root access is needed. Dependencies are already installed via `npm install -g yarn@1.22.22 && yarn install --ignore-scripts --ignore-engines`. Run tests with Jest, e.g. `node_modules/.bin/jest src/test/tooltip-interaction-behavior.spec.js`. The event-delegation logic lives under `src/components/Tooltip/`. Fix the library source; do not just add workarounds in test files.

message: `--dangerously-bypass-hook-trust` is enabled. Enabled hooks may run without review for this invocation.
`--dangerously-bypass-hook-trust` is enabled. Enabled hooks may run without review for this invocation.

text: The delegated implementation is isolated in `event-delegation.ts`, while `use-tooltip-events.tsx` supplies the anchor handlers. I’m tracing registration and cleanup together, including the tests that assert listener placement, so the fix preserves the library’s deduplication behavior.

text: The root cause is confirmed: `event-delegation.ts` installs one document listener per event type with the default bubbling phase. I’ll register the shared delegated listener in capture phase, and update removal with the same capture flag so cleanup remains correct; this lets the document observe the click before the anchor’s handler can stop propagation.

1	/**
     2	 * Shared document event delegation.
     3	 *
     4	 * Instead of N tooltips each calling document.addEventListener(type, handler),
     5	 * we maintain ONE document listener per event type. When the event fires,
     6	 * we iterate through all registered handlers for that type.
     7	 *
     8	 * This reduces document-level listeners from O(N × eventTypes) to O(eventTypes).
     9	 */
    10	
    11	type Handler = (event: Event) => void
    12	
    13	const handlersByType = new Map<string, Set<Handler>>()
    14	
    15	function getOrCreateSet(eventType: string): Set<Handler> {
    16	  let set = handlersByType.get(eventType)
    17	  if (!set) {
    18	    set = new Set()
    19	    handlersByType.set(eventType, set)
    20	    document.addEventListener(eventType, dispatch)
    21	  }
    22	  return set
    23	}
    24	
    25	function dispatch(event: Event): void {
    26	  const handlers = handlersByType.get(event.type)
    27	  if (handlers) {
    28	    // Safe to iterate directly — mutations (add/remove) only happen in
    29	    // setup/cleanup, not during dispatch. Set iteration is stable for
    30	    // entries that existed when iteration began.
    31	    handlers.forEach((handler) => {
    32	      handler(event)
    33	    })
    34	  }
    35	}
    36	
    37	/**
    38	 * Register a handler for a document-level event type.
    39	 * Returns an unsubscribe function.
    40	 */
    41	export function addDelegatedEventListener(eventType: string, handler: Handler): () => void {
    42	  const set = getOrCreateSet(eventType)
    43	  set.add(handler)
    44	
    45	  return () => {
    46	    set.delete(handler)
    47	    if (set.size === 0) {
    48	      handlersByType.delete(eventType)
    49	      document.removeEventListener(eventType, dispatch)
    50	    }
    51	  }
    52	}
    53	
    54	/**
    55	 * Reset for testing purposes.
    56	 */
    57	export function resetEventDelegation(): void {
    58	  handlersByType.forEach((_handlers, eventType) => {
    59	    document.removeEventListener(eventType, dispatch)
    60	  })
    61	  handlersByType.clear()
    62	}

text: I’m making the narrow source change now: the shared document listener will use capture phase consistently, and every corresponding removal will pass the same capture setting. No anchor-side workaround is needed, so the behavior applies to dynamically resolved anchors as well.
The source patch is in place and the focused Jest run is still completing. The diff is limited to the delegation module; the pre-existing `yarn.lock` modification remains untouched.

The focused suite passes all 8 tests. I’m running the full Jest suite and a TypeScript/type-bundle check next, since changing listener options affects shared registration and teardown across all tooltip interactions.

text: The imperative failure reproduces when that suite runs alone, so it is unrelated to this change; the tooltip is rendered but its existing `isOpen` assertion is already timing-sensitive. The regression test passes, and the changed delegation source passes ESLint. The focused suite now has 9 passing tests, including the propagation-stopping anchor case; I’m doing a final diff/status check to ensure only the intended source and regression test changed.

text:
Fixed delegated tooltip events to use capture phase, allowing `openOnClick` to work when anchors call `stopPropagation()`.

Added a regression test in [tooltip-interaction-behavior.spec.js](/home/user/workspace/src/test/tooltip-interaction-behavior.spec.js:71).

Verification:
- Focused Jest suite: 9 passed
- Type declaration build: passed
- ESLint: passed
- Full suite: 174 passed, 1 pre-existing imperative API failure unrelated to this change
